name.text: Calculating the area scale factor and the volume of a final enlarged sculpture.

questiontext.text:
A designer creates a prototype of a cylindrical fountain pedestal with a radius of \({@r_p@}\) m and a height of \({@h_p@}\) m.

  • The designer draws the pedestal on a blueprint using a scale factor of 1:50. What is the linear scale factor \(k\) for this reduction? [[input:ans1]] [[validation:ans1]] [[feedback:prt1]]

A sculptor is commissioned to create the final fountain by enlarging the prototype using a linear scale factor of \({@k_enlarge@}\).

[[jsxgraph width="500px" height="550px"]] var board = JXG.JSXGraph.initBoard(divid, { boundingbox: [-6, 15, 16, -2], axis: false, showCopyright: false }); var p = 0.3; /* STACK variables */ var r = {#r_p#}; var h = {#h_p#}; var k = {#k_enlarge#}; /* Prototype dimensions */ var Rp = r; var Hp = h; /* Enlarged dimensions (automatic scaling) */ var Re = k * r; var He = k * h; /* Positions */ var x1 = -3; var x2 = 3; /* ===================== PROTOTYPE CYLINDER ===================== */ // sides board.create('segment', [[x1-Rp,0],[x1-Rp,Hp]], {strokeColor:'#1f77b4', strokeWidth:3}); board.create('segment', [[x1+Rp,0],[x1+Rp,Hp]], {strokeColor:'#1f77b4', strokeWidth:3}); // base board.create('curve', [ function(t){return x1 + Rp*Math.cos(t)}, function(t){return (Rp*p)*Math.sin(t)}, Math.PI,2*Math.PI ], {strokeColor:'#1f77b4', strokeWidth:3}); // top board.create('curve', [ function(t){return x1 + Rp*Math.cos(t)}, function(t){return Hp + (Rp*p)*Math.sin(t)}, 0,2*Math.PI ], {strokeColor:'#1f77b4', strokeWidth:3}); board.create('text',[x1-1.5,Hp+1,"Prototype"]); /* ===================== ENLARGED CYLINDER ===================== */ // sides board.create('segment', [[x2-Re,0],[x2-Re,He]], {strokeColor:'#d62728', strokeWidth:3}); board.create('segment', [[x2+Re,0],[x2+Re,He]], {strokeColor:'#d62728', strokeWidth:3}); // base board.create('curve', [ function(t){return x2 + Re*Math.cos(t)}, function(t){return (Re*p)*Math.sin(t)}, Math.PI,2*Math.PI ], {strokeColor:'#d62728', strokeWidth:3}); // top board.create('curve', [ function(t){return x2 + Re*Math.cos(t)}, function(t){return He + (Re*p)*Math.sin(t)}, 0,2*Math.PI ], {strokeColor:'#d62728', strokeWidth:3}); board.create('text',[x2-2,He+1,"Final Sculpture (k = {#k_enlarge#})"]); [[/jsxgraph]]
  1. What is the Area Scale Factor for the final sculpture? [[input:ans2]] [[validation:ans2]] [[feedback:prt2]]
  2. If the prototype has a volume of {@vol_proto@} m³, calculate the volume of the final enlarged sculpture. [[input:ans3]] [[validation:ans3]] [[feedback:prt3]] m³

( Round your final volume to 2 decimal places.)

generalfeedback.text:
Worked Solution

\(\begin{align} \text{Linear Scale Factor (Reduction)} &= \frac{1}{50} = 0.02 \\ \text{Area Scale Factor} &= k^2 = {@k_enlarge@}^2 = {@asf@} \\ \text{Volume of Image} &= \text{Volume of Object} \times k^3 \ &= {@vol_proto@} \times {@k_enlarge@}^3 \ &= {@vol_proto@} \times {@vsf@} \ &= {@vol_final@} \text{ m}^3 \end{align}\)

prtcorrect.text: Correct answer, well done.

prtpartiallycorrect.text: Your answer is partially correct.

prtincorrect.text: Incorrect answer.
